name: Monstera egregia
origin: Central and southern Mexico to Belize
aboutText: Monstera egregia is a climbing epiphyte native to the wet tropical forests of Central and southern Mexico to Belize. It ascends trees using aerial roots, typically starting in the understory and growing towards the canopy. This species thrives in humid, shaded environments with rich, organic soils and frequent rainfall, developing a graceful, open appearance with age.

fieldNotes.title: Distinguished by Name
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Egregia means 'distinguished' or 'remarkable' — the taxonomist's assessment at the time of description. Whether it merits that distinction relative to other Central American Monstera is a fair question. From Mexico to Belize, producing moderately fenestrated adult leaves in humid forest conditions. Has attracted some collector attention among those working through the genus systematically. Priced at the upper middle tier, which positions it as a specialist purchase rather than an impulse buy, which is probably appropriate.
